layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — no quality swing trade setups available right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with a Fear & Greed score of 26, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.2%, which means money is flowing away from smaller coins toward Bitcoin. Most altcoin candidates on our watchlist are in downtrends with bearish chart structures and poor risk/reward ratios. While a couple of coins like TRUMP show oversold readings that could bounce, their overall charts are too ugly to justify risking capital. The smart move is to stay patient and wait for better setups.
layman_action: Stay in cash. The market is fearful and most altcoins are bleeding. Wait for clearer reversal signals before committing capital — patience here protects your portfolio.
expert_reasoning: Systematically evaluated all 10 candidates against swing trading criteria. TRUMP (RSI 29, oversold) is the closest to a buy signal but its chart bias is -0.70, below all MAs, in a persistent downtrend with -13% 5-day momentum — classic falling knife. HTX has the strongest chart bias (+1.10) but RSI at 58 flags correction risk and at a price of $0.00000185 with no funding rate or volume data, execution risk is extreme. GRASS dropped 25.5% in 7 days which could mean-revert, but a bearish engulfing pattern and -0.90 chart bias suggest further downside. ETHFI shows bullish marubozu and +0.60 bias but resistance at $0.40 is only 2.5% away — terrible risk/reward at 0.11x. High BTC dominance (56.2%) is hostile to alt positions. The Fear & Greed improving trend (24→26) is slightly constructive but not enough to override the lack of quality setups.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.2% continues to crush altcoin performance — any further BTC strength drains alt liquidity
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May signals capital leaving crypto entirely, reducing buy-side support
Geopolitical escalation (U.S.-Iran strikes) could trigger sudden risk-off selling across all crypto assets
watch_list: BTC behavior at the Fidelity power law support line — a bounce here would lift all boats and create alt entry points
TRUMP RSI approaching deeply oversold territory — if RSI drops below 25 with a hammer candle, could be a mean-reversion play
Fear & Greed index trend — currently improving from 24 to 26; a move above 30 would signal sentiment recovery and safer alt entries
market_context: The crypto market is in a fearful consolidation phase with BTC flat around $64K and high dominance squeezing altcoins. Macro headwinds including stablecoin outflows and geopolitical tensions are keeping risk appetite suppressed.
context_banner: Market fear and high BTC dominance create a hostile environment for alt swing trades — capital preservation is the optimal strategy.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 ('Fear') with improving trend from 24 — not yet sufficient for contrarian alt entries but worth monitoring for inflection above 30.
TRUMP RSI at 29 is oversold, historically a bounce zone per our 75% win rate on RSI signals, but bearish chart structure (-0.70 bias) invalidates the setup.
Stablecoin market cap shrinking by $10B since May signals capital outflows from crypto, reducing buy-side liquidity for swing trades.
Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market per Jamie Coutts suggests potential macro bottom forming, but not yet confirmed — premature to front-run.
Best chart bias among candidates is HTX at +1.10, but most actionable candidates show bearish structures (GRASS -0.90, TRUMP -0.70, VIRTUAL -0.70) making entries high-risk.
